key_insight: GLOW-FDG is an open-source deep learning model that automatically segments whole-body cancer lesions on FDG-PET/CT across multiple cancer types.
clinical_relevance: It could speed up and standardize tumor burden measurement for precision oncology workflows by reducing manual contouring effort.
method: Trained on 1,563 whole-body FDG-PET/CT scans from multiple cancer types
Evaluated on 185 external scans from independent institutions
Compared against publicly available benchmark models for lesion detection and segmentation
evidence_signal: In external testing across breast, lung, head and neck cancer, and melanoma, the model outperformed benchmarks, reduced false positives, and produced robust tumor burden and total lesion glycolysis estimates.
watch_item: Despite strong generalization in external cohorts, real-world performance may vary by scanner protocols, tracer uptake patterns, and case mix, so site-specific validation is important.
paper_title: GLOW-FDG: Generalized cancer LesiOn Whole-body segmentation model for $^{18}$F-FDG-PET/CT
